description: Fix two CLI UX issues found during fresh-user experience testing: (1) rotate-secret success message lacks guidance on viewing the new secret, (2) audit command shows misleading 'Authentication failed' when a non-OpenTabs service occupies the port.

userStories.title: Add actionable follow-up hint to rotate-secret success message
userStories.description: As a user who just rotated their secret, I want the success message to tell me how to view the new secret so that I can update my MCP client configs without guessing.
acceptanceCriteria: After `opentabs config rotate-secret --confirm` succeeds (server running), the output includes a line like: Run `opentabs config show --show-secret` to see MCP client configuration with the new secret.
After `opentabs config rotate-secret --confirm` succeeds (server NOT running), the output also includes the same hint about `opentabs config show --show-secret`
The hint appears on all success paths (lines 729-731, 734-736, 738-743, 746-747)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts, function handleRotateSecret (lines 694-749).

There are four success paths, each printing slightly different messages:
1. Lines 729-731: Server successfully notified. Currently says 'Update MCP client configs (Claude Code, OpenCode, etc.) with the new secret.' — add a follow-up line with the hint.
2. Lines 734-736: Server responded but non-OK status.
3. Lines 738-743: Server not reachable (connection refused or other error).
4. Lines 746-747: No old secret existed.

All four paths should include the hint. Add a single console.log line after each path's existing output: console.log(pc.dim('Run `opentabs config show --show-secret` to see MCP client configuration with the new secret.'));

Keep the style consistent with the existing output — use pc.dim() for the hint, matching the surrounding code.

userStories.title: Add non-OpenTabs service detection to audit command
userStories.description: As a user who accidentally has another service on port 9515, I want `opentabs audit` to tell me 'No OpenTabs server found' instead of the misleading 'Authentication failed' so that I understand the actual problem.
acceptanceCriteria: When a non-OpenTabs HTTP service on the target port returns a non-401 4xx or non-JSON content type, audit prints 'No OpenTabs server found on port <N>. The port may be in use by another service.' instead of 'Error: MCP server returned HTTP <status>'
When the server returns 401, audit still prints 'Authentication failed' (unchanged behavior — 401 means the service might be OpenTabs with a wrong secret)
The detection logic uses the same isNonOpenTabsHttpError() helper already used by the status command
